title: 10 best herbal teas for bad breath

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Peppermint teas helps with bad breath because its essential oils, particularly menthol, have natural antibacterial properties that effectively combat the bacteria responsible for bad breath. When consumed as a tea, peppermint's refreshing flavor and aroma help stimulate saliva production, which rinses away volatile sulfur compounds (VSCs) responsible for unpleasant odors. Additionally, peppermint's cooling sensation may help reduce inflammation in the mouth and throat, further alleviating halitosis symptoms. By drinking herbal peppermint teas regularly, individuals can enjoy a fresher breath and improved oral hygiene.
remedy_properties: Menthol: Menthol's primary property is its ability to reduce inflammation in the mouth and throat, which can contribute to bad breath by reducing bacteria growth and alleviating dry mouth.

Eugenol: Eugenol has antibacterial properties, making it effective against the bacteria that cause bad breath (halitosis) by inhibiting their growth and killing them off, thereby reducing volatile sulfur compounds in the mouth.

Cineole: Cineole, a terpene found in peppermint tea, has analgesic and anti-inflammatory effects, which can help to alleviate sore throats and gum irritation that may contribute to bad breath, as well as freshening the breath by reducing bacterial growth.
remedy_parts: Roots: Contain menthol, which helps freshen and deodorize the mouth, reducing bad breath.
Leaves: High in menthol and other compounds that help soothe and calm the digestive system, relieving symptoms of bad breath.
remedy_recipe: Measure out one teaspoon of dried peppermint leaves per eight ounces of boiling water.
Steep the peppermint leaves in the hot water for five to seven minutes to allow the flavors to meld.
Strain the tea mixture through a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth to remove the solids.
Add one tablespoon of honey to taste and stir until fully dissolved before serving.
Allow the tea to cool slightly before drinking to help freshen breath.

remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Kansas Coneflower tea helps reduce inflammation in the mouth, which can contribute to bad breath by reducing the production of volatile sulfur compounds (VSCs) that cause unpleasant odors.
Antimicrobial properties: The tea's antimicrobial properties help combat the growth of bacteria and other microorganisms that can cause bad breath by breaking down food particles and producing VSCs.

Antioxidant properties: Kansas Coneflower tea's antioxidant properties help protect against oxidative stress and damage to oral tissues, which can contribute to bad breath by reducing the body's ability to fight off infections and repair damaged tissues.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds that help reduce inflammation and eliminate bacteria responsible for bad breath.
Seeds: Containing potent antibacterial properties, seeds help kill the bacteria that cause halitosis (bad breath) and freshen the mouth.
remedy_recipe: Harvest dried echinacea angustifolia flowers in bulk quantities for future use.
Measure one teaspoon of dried flowers per cup of boiling water and steep for five minutes.
Strain the liquid using a tea infuser or fine-mesh sieve into a clean glass container.
Add honey to taste as desired, stirring well to dissolve any crystallized particles, then store in refrigerator up to three days.
Drink one to two cups of the herbal tea twice daily for optimal antibacterial and anti-inflammatory effects.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Kansas coneflower teas helps with bad breath because of its potent antimicrobial properties. The tea contains compounds like cinnamic acid, which has been shown to inhibit the growth of bacteria that cause halitosis. Additionally, the flavonoids and phenolic acids present in the tea have antibacterial and anti-inflammatory effects, helping to reduce plaque and gingivitis, two common causes of bad breath. By killing off these oral pathogens, herbal Kansas coneflower teas can help freshen breath and promote overall oral health.

Lemon balm teas helps with bad breath because of its natural antibacterial properties that effectively combat the underlying causes of bad breath, such as gum disease, oral thrush, and dry mouth. The tea's antiseptic qualities also help to reduce the growth of bacteria in the mouth, which is responsible for the unpleasant odors associated with bad breath. Additionally, lemon balm teas contain antioxidants that soothe and calm the gums, promoting a healthy mouth environment that is less prone to bad breath.
remedy_properties: Antimicrobial properties: Lemon balm tea's antimicrobial properties help combat the growth of bacteria that can cause bad breath by inhibiting the reproduction of these microorganisms.
Antioxidant properties: The antioxidants present in lemon balm tea help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the mouth, which can contribute to bad breath and gum disease.

Anti-inflammatory properties: Lemon balm tea's anti-inflammatory properties help alleviate swelling and redness in the gums and mucous membranes, reducing the likelihood of bad breath caused by irritated oral tissues.
remedy_parts: Roots: Lemon balm roots have been traditionally used to reduce oral inflammation and combat bad breath due to their anti-inflammatory properties.
Leaves: The leaves of the lemon balm plant contain rosmarinic acid, which has natural antibacterial properties that help eliminate bacteria causing bad breath.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh melissa officinalis leaves or use dried ones of good quality weighing about two tablespoons for one cup water.
Boil one cup of water in a tea kettle or pot for at least five minutes to ensure it reaches a rolling boil temperature.
Pour the boiling water over the lemon balm leaves allowing them to steep for seven to ten minutes to release their active compounds.
Strain the tea into a cup using a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth squeezing out as much liquid as possible discarding solids.
Allow the tea to cool slightly then drink it hot or let it cool down to enjoy over ice if preferred taking one to two cups daily for optimal benefits.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Thyme teas helps with bad breath because of its potent antibacterial properties, which effectively eliminate odor-causing bacteria in the mouth. The tea's essential oils, particularly thymol and carvacrol, work to reduce volatile sulfur compounds (VSCs) that are responsible for bad breath. As a natural anti-inflammatory, thyme tea also soothes gum inflammation and reduces plaque buildup, further contributing to fresher, cleaner breath. By drinking thyme teas regularly, individuals can enjoy a more confident smile with a reduced risk of halitosis.
remedy_properties: Antimicrobial Properties: Thyme tea's antimicrobial properties help combat bacteria and other microorganisms that can cause bad breath by inhibiting their growth and reducing their ability to produce volatile sulfur compounds.

Anti-inflammatory Properties: The anti-inflammatory properties of thyme tea may help reduce swelling and irritation in the mouth, throat, and gums, which can contribute to bad breath, as well as alleviate symptoms like gum inflammation and bleeding.

Antioxidant Properties: Thyme tea's high antioxidant content helps protect against oxidative stress and damage to oral tissues, which can contribute to bad breath by reducing the body's ability to fight off infections and repair damaged tissues.
remedy_parts: Roots: Thyme roots are often used in tea blends because they contain compounds that help reduce inflammation and bacteria growth, which can contribute to bad breath.
Leaves: Fresh or dried thyme leaves are a popular choice for making teas that combat bad breath due to their natural antibacterial properties.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh or dried thymus vulgaris leaves in sufficient quantity for the desired amount of tea.
Combine one teaspoon of crushed thyme leaves with one cup of boiling water and let steep for five minutes.
Strain the tea mixture through a fine-mesh sieve into a cup to remove loose thyme particles.
Allow the tea to cool to room temperature or drink it warm after a few minutes of steeping.
Consume the herbal thyme tea immediately after preparation, ideally before brushing teeth and going about daily activities.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Wild marjoram teas helps with bad breath because of its natural antibacterial properties that target the underlying causes of halitosis. The tea's active compounds, such as rosmarinic acid and camphor, work to reduce the growth of bacteria in the mouth and throat, thereby eliminating the sources of unpleasant odors. Additionally, marjoram's anti-inflammatory properties help soothe and calm irritated mucous membranes, promoting a healthy oral environment that fosters fresh breath.
remedy_properties: Antimicrobial properties: Wild marjoram tea's antimicrobial properties can help combat the bacteria that cause bad breath by inhibiting their growth and reducing the formation of volatile sulfur compounds.
Antioxidant properties: The antioxidants present in wild marjoram tea can help neutralize free radicals that contribute to gum inflammation, which is often a underlying factor of bad breath.
Anti-inflammatory properties: Wild marjoram's anti-inflammatory properties can help reduce swelling and irritation in the mouth and gums, which can also contribute to bad breath.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of wild marjoram have anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce swelling and redness in the mouth, making them an effective ingredient in teas for bad breath.
Leaves: Wild marjoram leaves have antibacterial properties that combat the bacteria responsible for bad breath, leaving a fresh and clean taste.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh wild marjoram leaves and flowers, or purchase dried herbs in the morning for maximum potency.
Measure one teaspoon of dried wild marjoram or two teaspoons of fresh leaves and flowers into a heat-resistant teapot or infuser.
Pour 8 ounces of boiling water over the herb mixture and let it steep for 5-7 minutes to allow flavors and compounds to extract.
Strain the tea into a cup using a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth, discarding solids and retaining liquid, then add honey to taste if desired.
Allow the tea to cool slightly before drinking, as the warm liquid will help open up nasal passages and promote oral cleansing.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Ceylon cinnamon teas helps with bad breath because of its natural antibacterial properties. The essential oils present in Ceylon cinnamon, such as cinnamaldehyde, have been shown to inhibit the growth of bacteria that cause bad breath. Additionally, the tea's warm and soothing effects help to stimulate saliva production, which can help to neutralize acids and reduce volatile sulfur compounds that contribute to bad breath. By drinking Ceylon cinnamon teas regularly, individuals may experience a noticeable reduction in their bad breath.
remedy_properties: Antimicrobial properties: Ceylon cinnamon's antimicrobial properties help to combat the growth of bacteria and other microorganisms in the mouth, reducing the likelihood of bad breath.
Antioxidant properties: Ceylon cinnamon's antioxidant properties help to neutralize free radicals that can contribute to gum disease and other oral health issues that can cause bad breath.
Anti-inflammatory properties: Ceylon cinnamon's anti-inflammatory properties help to reduce swelling and inflammation in the mouth and throat, which can be a contributing factor to bad breath.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in antioxidants and antibacterial compounds, roots help combat bacteria that cause bad breath.
Rhyzomes: With its anti-inflammatory properties, rhyzomes soothe gum inflammation and reduce bacterial growth that leads to bad breath.
Barks: The barks' antimicrobial properties help eliminate odor-causing bacteria and freshen the mouth.
remedy_recipe: Gather one teaspoon of dried ceylon cinnamon bark.
Boil one cup of water in a saucepan for five minutes.
Add the cinnamon bark to the boiling water and steep for seven minutes.
Strain the tea through a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth into a teacup.
Serve the tea hot with a sweetener such as honey if desired.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Sage teas helps with bad breath because of its antibacterial and anti-inflammatory properties. The tea's active compounds, such as camphor and borneol, have been shown to inhibit the growth of bacteria that can cause bad breath. Additionally, sage's astringent properties help to reduce inflammation in the mouth and throat, which can also contribute to halitosis. As a result, drinking herbal sage teas may help to freshen breath and leave you feeling more confident with your oral health.
remedy_properties: Antimicrobial properties: Sage tea's antimicrobial properties help to reduce the growth of bacteria in the mouth, throat, and digestive tract, which can contribute to bad breath.
Antioxidant properties: The antioxidants in sage tea help to neutralize free radicals and reduce oxidative stress, which can damage oral tissues and lead to bad breath.
Anti-inflammatory properties: Sage tea's anti-inflammatory properties may help to reduce inflammation in the mouth and throat, which can cause swelling and irritation that contributes to bad breath.
remedy_parts: Roots: Sage roots have been traditionally used to make teas for their antibacterial and anti-inflammatory properties, which help combat bad breath.
Barks: Sage bark contains salubrinal, a compound that has been shown to reduce volatile sulfur compounds in the mouth, contributing to fresh breath.
remedy_recipe: Harvest one tablespoon of dried sage leaves for every 8 ounces of boiling water.
Combine the sage leaves with 8 ounces of boiling water in a teapot or infuser.
Steep the mixture for 5-7 minutes, allowing the sage to release its active compounds.
Strain the tea into a cup using a cheesecloth or fine-mesh sieve to remove the solids.
Drink the tea immediately, savoring the earthy flavor and potential benefits for bad breath.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Rosemary teas helps with bad breath because its natural antibacterial properties help to reduce the amount of bacteria that can cause halitosis. The tea's potent compounds, such as camphor and bornyl acetate, work to kill off unwanted germs that can linger in the mouth and digestive tract. As a result, rosemary teas can leave the breath feeling fresher and cleaner, providing a natural and effective solution for alleviating bad breath.
remedy_properties: Antimicrobial properties: Rosemary's antimicrobial compounds, such as camphor and borneol, help combat the bacteria that cause bad breath by inhibiting their growth and killing them off.
Anti-inflammatory properties: Rosemary's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as rosmarinic acid, reduce inflammation in the mouth and gums, which can contribute to bad breath and gum disease.
Antioxidant properties: Rosemary's antioxidants help protect the body from free radicals that can damage oral tissues and contribute to bad breath by reducing oxidative stress.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rosemary roots have antimicrobial properties, making them effective in reducing bacteria and fungi that cause bad breath.
Seeds: Rosemary seeds contain antioxidants and flavonoids that help reduce inflammation and eliminate odor-causing compounds from the mouth.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh rosemary leaves from your garden or store-dried rosemary in an airtight container.
Measure out two teaspoons of dried rosemary leaves for every eight ounces of boiling water.
Heat water in a teapot or kettle and let it boil for one minute to ensure sterilization.
Steep the dried rosemary leaves in the boiling water for five to seven minutes, then strain with a fine-mesh sieve.
Drink the tea immediately after straining, allowing the antimicrobial properties of rosemary to combat bad breath.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Licorice teas helps with bad breath because of its natural antibacterial properties. The tea's active compounds, such as glycyrrhizin and licoricidin, inhibit the growth of bacteria in the mouth, which is a major cause of bad breath. Additionally, the tea's soothing effects can help reduce inflammation in the gums and tongue, which can contribute to bad breath. By killing bacteria and reducing inflammation, herbal licorice teas can leave your mouth feeling fresh and clean, eliminating bad breath for good.
remedy_properties: Antibacterial and antiviral compounds: Licorice root contains triterpenoid saponins, which have been shown to inhibit the growth of bacteria and viruses that can cause bad breath.

Anti-inflammatory effects: Licorice root's flavonoids and phenolic acids help reduce inflammation in the mouth and throat, which can alleviate symptoms like bad breath caused by gum disease or other oral infections.

Antioxidant properties: Licorice tea contains antioxidants that help neutralize free radicals and reduce oxidative stress, which can contribute to the production of volatile sulfur compounds (VSCs) responsible for bad breath.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in glycyrrhizin, a compound that helps reduce inflammation and fights off bacteria responsible for bad breath.
Bark: Contains a compound called isoliquiritigenin, which has antibacterial properties that help combat oral infections causing bad breath.
remedy_recipe: Measure out one teaspoon of dried glycyrrhizae root and place it in a teapot or infuser.
Boil one cup of water and pour it over the glycyrrhizae root, allowing it steep for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the tea leaves from the liquid using a spoon or strainer, discarding the solids.
Add one tablespoon of honey to the tea if desired, stirring until dissolved, then serve hot or warm.
Drink the licorice tea twice daily, morning and evening, for at least 7 days to achieve optimal benefits.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Ginger teas helps with bad breath because of its natural antibacterial and anti-inflammatory properties. The antioxidants in ginger, such as gingerol and shogaol, have been shown to reduce the growth of bacteria that can cause bad breath, also known as halitosis. Additionally, ginger's anti-inflammatory effects can help soothe and calm the mucous membranes in the mouth and throat, reducing inflammation and discomfort associated with bad breath. This makes ginger teas a natural and effective way to combat bad breath and promote fresh, clean breath.
remedy_properties: Antimicrobial properties: Ginger tea's antimicrobial properties help to kill bacteria that can cause bad breath by reducing the growth of harmful microorganisms in the mouth and throat.
Anti-inflammatory properties: The anti-inflammatory compounds present in ginger tea reduce inflammation and swelling in the mouth, gums, and throat, which can contribute to bad breath.

Digestive benefits: Ginger tea's digestive benefits help to alleviate digestive issues like indigestion, nausea, and bloating, which can contribute to bad breath by causing stomach acid to flow back up into the mouth.
remedy_parts: Rhyzomes: The rhyzome, which is the underground stem of the ginger plant, has anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce inflammation in the mouth and eliminate bacteria that cause bad breath.
Roots: Ginger roots contain compounds like gingerol and shogaol that have antibacterial and antifungal properties, making them effective against the microbes that contribute to bad breath.
remedy_recipe: Rinse fresh ginger root under cold running water to remove dirt and impurities.
Grate one-inch piece of fresh ginger using a fine grater for optimal flavor release.
Combine grated ginger with boiling water in a medium-sized teapot or cup.
Steep the ginger mixture for five minutes to allow flavors and compounds to infuse.
Strain and serve warm, discarding ginger solids after three to five minutes of steeping time.

intro_desc: Herbal teas for bad breath are a natural and effective way to combat halitosis, or chronic bad breath. These teas contain antioxidants and antimicrobial properties that help kill bacteria in the mouth and freshen breath. Examples of herbal teas that aid in bad breath relief include peppermint, chamomile, and fennel tea. Drinking these teas can improve one's quality of life by boosting confidence and reducing social anxiety related to bad breath.
supplementary_best_treatment: The best way to treat bad breath with herbal teas is to drink a cup of peppermint tea after meals. Peppermint's antibacterial properties help reduce bacteria in the mouth that cause bad breath. Another option is to try sage tea, which has natural decongestant properties that can help dry up excess saliva and kill bacteria. Both teas are easy to find and brew at home.
supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to bad breath that are treated with herbal teas are digestive issues such as bloating, gas, and indigestion. Herbal teas like peppermint, ginger, and chamomile have anti-inflammatory properties that help soothe the digestive tract and reduce symptoms of irritable bowel syndrome (IBS). Additionally, teas like fennel and dill have carminative properties that can relieve bloating and gas, providing relief from discomfort and unpleasant odors.
